message:
feat(tutorial): implement responsive tutorial layout with context provider (e521)

Add TutorialContext and responsive layout for tutorial pages with three
breakpoint-aware views and localStorage progress persistence.

## TutorialContext Provider (lib/tutorial-context.tsx)

State management for tutorial progress:
- currentPath, currentStep, completedSteps tracking
- Navigation: goToStep, goToNextStep, goToPrevStep
- Progress: markStepComplete, completeAndAdvance
- Path management: setPath, resetProgress, clearAllProgress
- Computed: canGoNext, canGoBack, progressPercent

### Persistence
- localStorage key pattern: brenner-tutorial-{pathId}-progress
- Auto-hydration on mount with validation
- Debounced persistence on state changes
- Cross-tab synchronization via storage events

### Hooks
- useTutorial() - throws if outside provider
- useTutorialOptional() - returns null if outside provider

## Responsive Layout (app/tutorial/layout.tsx)

Three breakpoint configurations:

### Desktop (≥1024px)
- Fixed 288px sidebar with full step list
- Uses SidebarProgress component
- Back to Tutorial link in header
- Progress summary in footer

### Tablet (768-1023px)
- Collapsed 64px icon sidebar
- Dot indicators for steps (color-coded)
- Hover tooltips for step titles
- Auto-collapses based on window resize

### Mobile (<768px)
- Header progress bar (Step X of Y)
- Animated progress line
- Hamburger menu with slide-out panel
- Touch-friendly 44px targets
- Spring animation for menu transitions

## Path Configurations

Pre-defined step metadata for all three paths:
- quick-start: 7 steps (~30 min)
- agent-assisted: 8 steps (~65 min)
- multi-agent: 10 steps (~90 min)

Each path includes step IDs, titles, and time estimates.

## Layout Behavior

- Landing pages (/tutorial/quick-start): No sidebar, centered content
- Step pages (/tutorial/quick-start/step/1): Full sidebar layout
- Auto-close mobile menu on route change
- Proper z-indexing for overlays (z-40, z-50)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"brenner_bot-e00","title":"EPIC: Corpus Browser & Document Viewer","description":"# Purpose

The corpus is the foundation of everything - 236 transcript segments plus metaprompts, quote bank, and other documents. Users need to:

1. **Discover** - Browse all documents, filter by type, search by content
2. **Navigate** - Jump to specific sections via TOC, use stable §n anchors
3. **Read** - Beautiful typography, syntax highlighting, comfortable reading experience
4. **Cite** - Copy section references, build excerpt blocks for sessions

# Pages

1. **/corpus** - Document listing with cards, search bar, filters
2. **/corpus/[doc]** - Full document view with TOC sidebar, anchors, citation copy

# Components Needed

- **DocumentCard** - Thumbnail card with title, description, metadata
- **SearchBar** - Full-text search with preview results
- **MarkdownViewer** - Rich renderer with TOC generation, anchor links
- **CopyButton** - For copying citations/excerpts
- **TableOfContents** - Sticky sidebar TOC with scroll highlighting

# Technical Decisions

- Server components for document loading (better SEO, faster initial load)
- Client-side search with prebuilt index (fast, works offline)
- Use rehype/remark plugins for markdown processing
- Generate TOC from heading structure
- §n anchors should be clickable and copyable

# Depends On

 {"id":"brenner_bot-e0m","title":"Bug: harden release workflow dispatch + edge proxy env access","description":"Follow-up hardening discovered during QA.

1) Release workflow safety:
- .github/workflows/release.yml currently gates publish vs dry-run using whether github.ref starts with refs/tags/.
- A workflow_dispatch run against a tag ref could therefore publish release assets.
- Desired behavior: publish only on tag push events; treat all workflow_dispatch runs as dry-runs (build + upload dist artifact only).

2) Edge proxy robustness:
- apps/web/src/proxy.ts should avoid dynamic env access (process.env["BRENNER_TRUST_CF_ACCESS_HEADERS"]).
- In Edge/middleware bundling, dynamic env access can be unreliable; use process.env.BRENNER_TRUST_CF_ACCESS_HEADERS instead.

3) Minor docs/test clarity:
- apps/web/src/lib/artifact-merge.test.ts comment should align with repo rule: run web tests via "cd apps/web && bun run test" (not bun test).

Acceptance:
- workflow_dispatch never publishes release assets (even if run on a tag ref).
- Tag pushes (push on refs/tags/v*) still publish release assets.
- Proxy env gating remains correct and tests pass.

 {"id":"brenner_bot-e4sb","title":"Task: Implement Test Storage Layer","description":"# Task: Implement Test Storage Layer

## Objective
Create file-based storage for test records with cross-session indexing.

## Implementation Details

### File Location
`apps/web/src/lib/test-storage.ts`

### Storage Structure
```
.research/
├── tests/
│   ├── RS-20251230-tests.json
│   └── ...
└── test-index.json
```

### Per-Session File Format
```json
{
  "sessionId": "RS-20251230",
  "tests": [
    {
      "id": "T-RS20251230-001",
      "description": "Lineage tracing with Cre-lox to track cell fate",
      "targetHypotheses": [
        {
          "hypothesisId": "H-RS20251230-001",
          "prediction": "If epigenetically determined, daughter cells maintain parental methylation patterns"
        },
        {
          "hypothesisId": "H-RS20251230-002",
          "prediction": "If transcriptionally determined, daughter cells show variable expression patterns"
        }
      ],
      "designedInSession": "RS-20251230",
      "status": "completed",
      "execution": {
        "executedAt": "2025-12-31T10:00:00Z",
        "result": "Daughter cells showed variable methylation but stable expression",
        "matchedPredictions": [],
        "violatedPredictions": ["H-RS20251230-001", "H-RS20251230-002"],
        "confidence": "medium"
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

### Core Functions
```typescript
// CRUD operations
export async function loadSessionTests(sessionId: string): Promise<TestRecord[]>
export async function saveSessionTests(sessionId: string, tests: TestRecord[]): Promise<void>
export async function getTestById(id: string): Promise<TestRecord | null>

// Index operations
export async function rebuildTestIndex(): Promise<void>
export async function getTestsForHypothesis(hypothesisId: string): Promise<TestRecord[]>

// Query operations
export async function getTestsByStatus(status: TestStatus): Promise<TestRecord[]>
export async function getBlockedTests(): Promise<TestRecord[]>
export async function getDesignedButNotExecutedTests(): Promise<TestRecord[]>
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test records stored in per-session JSON files
- [ ] Index enables lookup by hypothesis ID
- [ ] Query by status (designed, completed, blocked)
- [ ] Execution records embedded in test records
- [ ] Git-friendly format (one test per logical unit)

## Dependencies
- Task: Define Test Record and Execution Schemas

## Effort Estimate
~180 lines of code, ~2 hours","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:46:55.014699072-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-31T01:14:49.889455502-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-31T01:14:49.889455502-05:00","close_reason":"Test Storage Layer fully implemented in test-storage.ts with 35 passing tests. Includes CRUD operations, cross-session indexing, query operations by status/hypothesis/quality, statistics, and hypothesis coverage tracking.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-e4sb","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-m6yw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:49:02.032397647-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-e4sb","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-x0o2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:49:02.191083392-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-e4sb","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-jcuz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T22:41:28.805743428-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"brenner_bot-e66","title":"Create OKLCH color token system in globals.css","description":"# Purpose

Define the color palette for Brenner Bot using OKLCH color space. **Light theme is the default** with optional dark mode toggle.

# Color Tokens to Define

## Light Theme (Default)

```css
:root {
  /* Core */
  --background: oklch(0.99 0.005 90);      /* Warm off-white, like aged paper */
  --foreground: oklch(0.15 0.02 270);      /* Deep blue-black for text */
  
  /* Card/Surface */
  --card: oklch(1 0 0);                    /* Pure white for cards */
  --card-foreground: oklch(0.15 0.02 270);
  
  /* Primary - Academic blue (scholarly, trustworthy) */
  --primary: oklch(0.45 0.15 250);         /* Deep scholarly blue */
  --primary-foreground: oklch(0.98 0 0);
  
  /* Secondary - Warm paper tones */
  --secondary: oklch(0.96 0.02 85);        /* Light warm tan */
  --secondary-foreground: oklch(0.25 0.02 85);
  
  /* Accent - Brenner amber (for quotes, highlights) */
  --accent: oklch(0.75 0.15 70);           /* Warm amber */
  --accent-foreground: oklch(0.2 0.05 70);
  
  /* Muted - For secondary text */
  --muted: oklch(0.95 0.01 270);
  --muted-foreground: oklch(0.45 0.02 270);
  
  /* Model Colors (for distillation comparison) */
  --opus: oklch(0.65 0.2 30);              /* Warm orange */
  --gpt: oklch(0.55 0.18 250);             /* Cool blue */
  --gemini: oklch(0.6 0.2 180);            /* Teal/cyan */
  
  /* Semantic */
  --success: oklch(0.6 0.18 145);          /* Green */
  --warning: oklch(0.75 0.15 70);          /* Amber */
  --destructive: oklch(0.55 0.22 25);      /* Red */
}
```

## Dark Theme (Toggle)

```css
.dark {
  --background: oklch(0.15 0.02 270);      /* Deep blue-black */
  --foreground: oklch(0.92 0.01 90);       /* Warm off-white */
  --card: oklch(0.2 0.02 270);
  --card-foreground: oklch(0.92 0.01 90);
  --primary: oklch(0.7 0.15 250);          /* Brighter blue */
  --secondary: oklch(0.25 0.02 85);
  --accent: oklch(0.75 0.15 70);
  --muted: oklch(0.25 0.01 270);
  --muted-foreground: oklch(0.65 0.02 270);
}
```

# Design Rationale

**Why Light Default?**
- Primary use case is reading long-form text (transcripts, distillations)
- Academic/scholarly aesthetic (like reading a well-typeset book)
- Better for daytime reading and screenshots
- Dark mode available for night owls and terminal-lovers

**Why OKLCH?**
- Perceptually uniform (colors look equally vibrant)
- Wider gamut (more vivid colors on modern displays)
- Easier to maintain consistent lightness across palette
- Modern CSS standard with good browser support

# Implementation Notes

1. Add theme toggle component (top-right nav)
2. Persist preference in localStorage
3. Respect prefers-color-scheme on first visit
4. Smooth transition when toggling (200ms)

# Files to Create/Modify

- `apps/web/src/app/globals.css` - Token definitions
- `apps/web/src/components/theme-toggle.tsx` - Toggle button
- `apps/web/src/lib/theme.ts` - Theme utilities","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:12:53.916389604-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T19:34:12.065558108-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T19:34:12.065558108-05:00","close_reason":"OKLCH color tokens complete - globals.css has full palette with model colors","labels":["colors","design-system","ux","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-e66","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-7yz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:13:46.244443019-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-e66","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-1qx","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:21:59.386814311-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"brenner_bot-njiu","title":"TASK: Create Devil's Advocate Agent Persona","description":"
## The Missing Agent Persona

We have explicit persona tasks for:
- Brenner Channeler (xlk2.5) - channels Sydney Brenner's wisdom
- Experiment Designer (oytk) - helps design discriminative tests

But the Devil's Advocate - perhaps the MOST important agent - lacks an explicit persona definition task.

### Agent Role
**Devil's Advocate** - Actively challenges hypotheses, finds weaknesses, plays the skeptic

### System Prompt Elements
- "You are a rigorous scientific skeptic"
- "Your job is to CHALLENGE, not confirm"
- "Find the weakest assumption in every hypothesis"
- "Ask: What would have to be true for this to be false?"
- "Never accept an explanation without probing its foundations"
- "Channel Brenner: 'Before you fall in love with your hypothesis, try to kill it'"

### Key Behaviors
1. **Identify unstated assumptions** - What does the user take for granted?
2. **Find alternative explanations** - What else could produce this pattern?
3. **Attack the mechanism** - Why would X actually cause Y?
4. **Question the evidence** - Is the test really discriminative?
5. **Expose blind spots** - What isn't the user considering?

### Tone Calibration
- Firm but constructive (not cruel)
- Socratic questioning (not lecturing)
- Focuses on the idea, not the person
- Offers paths forward after challenges

### Interaction Pattern
User: "I hypothesize that X causes Y because Z"
Devil's Advocate:
- "What if Z causes both X and Y? (confounding)"
- "Have you considered that Y might cause X? (reverse causation)"
- "Your mechanism Z assumes [implicit assumption]. Is that justified?"
- "Even if X→Z→Y, would that be the ONLY pathway?"

### Integration
- Invoked during Hypothesis Intake (challenge early)
- Invoked before locking predictions (stress test)
- Invoked after supporting evidence (prevent confirmation bias)
- Works WITH Experiment Designer (challenge their test designs too)

### Depends On
- xlk2.1 (Agent Roles & System Prompts)
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T03:16:44.767503558-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T03:16:44.767503558-05:00","labels":["brenner-loop","devils-advocate","multi-agent"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-njiu","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-xlk2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T03:18:32.268910046-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-njjo","title":"EPIC: Evidence Ledger - Test Tracking & Belief Updates","description":"# Evidence Ledger - Test Tracking & Belief Updates

## Purpose
Track discriminative tests over time, record results, and update hypothesis confidence in a structured, auditable way.

## The Problem This Solves
Researchers gather evidence informally. They read papers, run experiments, have conversations. But they rarely track:
- What specific predictions did my hypothesis make?
- What tests did I run (or find in literature)?
- Did those tests discriminate between hypotheses?
- How should my confidence change?

The Evidence Ledger operationalizes the Brenner insight that **evidence should update beliefs proportionally to its discriminative power**.

## Core Data Structures

### EvidenceEntry
```typescript
interface EvidenceEntry {
  id: string;
  sessionId: string;
  hypothesisVersion: string;
  
  // The test
  test: {
    description: string;
    type: 'natural_experiment' | 'controlled_study' | 'cross_context' | 'mechanism_block' | 'dose_response' | 'literature' | 'observation';
    discriminativePower: 1 | 2 | 3 | 4 | 5;  // ★ rating
  };
  
  // Predictions
  predictionIfTrue: string;   // What we expected if H correct
  predictionIfFalse: string;  // What we expected if H wrong
  
  // Result
  result: 'supports' | 'challenges' | 'inconclusive';
  observation: string;
  source?: string;  // Citation or reference
  
  // Impact
  confidenceBefore: number;
  confidenceAfter: number;
  interpretation: string;
  
  // Metadata
  recordedAt: Date;
  recordedBy?: string;
}
```

### ConfidenceUpdate
The key insight: not all evidence is equal. A ★★★★★ test that fails to support has much more impact than a ★☆☆☆☆ test.

```typescript
function computeConfidenceUpdate(
  current: number,
  test: Test,
  result: 'supports' | 'challenges' | 'inconclusive'
): number {
  // Bayesian-ish update
  const power = test.discriminativePower / 5;  // 0.2 to 1.0
  
  if (result === 'supports') {
    // Weak update toward 100
    return current + (100 - current) * power * 0.2;
  } else if (result === 'challenges') {
    // Strong update toward 0
    return current - current * power * 0.4;
  } else {
    // No change
    return current;
  }
}
```

## UI Components

### Evidence Timeline
Visual timeline showing:
- Each test recorded
- Impact on confidence (arrow up/down)
- Running confidence score
- Color-coded by result

### Test Recording Modal
When user records new evidence:
1. What test did you run/find?
2. What did you expect if true?
3. What did you expect if false?
4. What did you observe?
5. How should we interpret this?

### Confidence Dashboard
- Current confidence: 37%
- Tests run: 5
- Survived: 2
- Challenged: 2
- Inconclusive: 1
- Robustness assessment: "Under significant pressure"

## Why This Matters
The Brenner approach is about accumulating discriminative evidence. Without a ledger:
- People forget what tests they considered
- Confirmation bias creeps in (remember supports, forget challenges)
- Confidence becomes vibes rather than evidence-based

With the ledger:
- Intellectual honesty is enforced
- The audit trail is visible
- Confidence is earned, not assumed

## Gamification Elements (Careful)
We could add:
- "Test Streak" - consecutive valid tests recorded
- "Robustness Badge" - hypothesis survives 5 strong tests
- "Scientific Humility" - hypothesis revised after challenges

But: gamification shouldn't encourage gaming the system. Design carefully.

## Dependencies
- Hypothesis Engine (tracks hypothesis versions)
- Operator Application (generates initial tests)

## Success Criteria
- Users can record evidence from any source
- Confidence updates feel fair and explicable
- The history is inspectable and auditable
- Users develop better intuitions about evidence quality

 {"id":"brenner_bot-njjo.2","title":"Implement Confidence Update Algorithm","description":"# Implement Confidence Update Algorithm

## Purpose
Create the algorithm that updates hypothesis confidence based on evidence, weighted by discriminative power.

## The Problem
Naive confidence updates treat all evidence equally. But:
- A ★★★★★ test that fails is devastating
- A ★☆☆☆☆ test that fails is barely informative
- Supporting evidence from weak tests shouldn't inflate confidence

## Algorithm Design
```typescript
function computeConfidenceUpdate(
  currentConfidence: number,
  test: { discriminativePower: 1 | 2 | 3 | 4 | 5 },
  result: 'supports' | 'challenges' | 'inconclusive'
): { newConfidence: number; delta: number; explanation: string } {
  
  const power = test.discriminativePower / 5;  // 0.2 to 1.0
  
  if (result === 'inconclusive') {
    return {
      newConfidence: currentConfidence,
      delta: 0,
      explanation: 'No clear result; confidence unchanged.'
    };
  }
  
  if (result === 'supports') {
    // Weak update toward 100
    // Strong tests provide more evidence, but supporting
    // evidence is inherently weaker than challenging evidence
    const delta = (100 - currentConfidence) * power * 0.15;
    return {
      newConfidence: Math.min(currentConfidence + delta, 99),
      delta,
      explanation: \`★\${'★'.repeat(test.discriminativePower - 1)} test supports hypothesis. Confidence ↑ \${delta.toFixed(1)}%\`
    };
  }
  
  if (result === 'challenges') {
    // Strong update toward 0
    // Discriminative tests that challenge are very informative
    const delta = currentConfidence * power * 0.3;
    return {
      newConfidence: Math.max(currentConfidence - delta, 1),
      delta: -delta,
      explanation: \`★\${'★'.repeat(test.discriminativePower - 1)} test challenges hypothesis. Confidence ↓ \${delta.toFixed(1)}%\`
    };
  }
}
```

## Asymmetry is Intentional
Per Brenner: It's easier to disprove than prove. Challenging evidence has more impact than supporting evidence.

## Edge Cases
- Confidence at 100: can still go down
- Confidence at 0: basically falsified, minimal recovery
- Multiple rapid updates: apply sequentially

## File Location
`apps/web/src/lib/brenner-loop/confidence.ts`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Algorithm matches Bayesian intuitions
- [ ] Asymmetry toward challenging evidence
- [ ] Explanations are clear
- [ ] Edge cases handled

 {"id":"brenner_bot-njjo.3","title":"Build Evidence Recording UI","description":"# Build Evidence Recording UI

## Purpose
Create the interface for users to record evidence (tests run, literature found, observations made).

## Flow

### Step 1: Select or Create Test
If from Exclusion Test phase:
> "Which test are you recording results for?"
> [Dropdown of generated tests]

Or create new:
> [Add Custom Test]

### Step 2: Record Predictions
If predictions not already set:
> "What did you predict if true?"
> [                                ]
> "What did you predict if false?"
> [                                ]

### Step 3: Record Observation
> "What did you actually observe?"
> [                                ]
> 
> Source/Citation (optional):
> [                                ]

### Step 4: Classify Result
> "How does this observation relate to your predictions?"
> 
> ○ Supports the hypothesis (matches prediction-if-true)
> ○ Challenges the hypothesis (matches prediction-if-false)
> ○ Inconclusive (doesn't clearly match either)

### Step 5: Interpret
> "How should we interpret this?"
> [                                ]

### Step 6: Confirm Update
Show the confidence update:
```
Confidence: 72% → 49% (−23%)

Interpretation: "★★★★★ test challenges hypothesis.
Algorithm change timing doesn't match depression rise.
Suggests reverse causation or third variable."

[Confirm] [Edit]
```

## Component API
```typescript
interface EvidenceRecorderProps {
  session: Session;
  predefinedTests: ExclusionTest[];
  onRecordEvidence: (entry: EvidenceEntry) => void;
}
```

## File Location
`apps/web/src/components/brenner-loop/evidence/EvidenceRecorder.tsx`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Multi-step recording works
- [ ] Can use generated tests or add custom
- [ ] Confidence update preview shown
- [ ] Interpretation field captured

 {"id":"brenner_bot-njjo.4","title":"Create Evidence Timeline Visualization","description":"# Create Evidence Timeline Visualization

## Purpose
Show the evidence accumulation over time with confidence changes.

## Design
```
Evidence Timeline
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Confidence: ████████████░░░░░░░░ 49%

Timeline:
                                                    NOW
    ─────●─────────●─────────●─────────●────────●────▶
         │         │         │         │        │
         │         │         │         │        │
         75%       60%       72%       49%      49%
    (start)   (−15%)   (+12%)    (−23%)    (±0%)
              ★★★★     ★★★★★    ★★★★★      ★★
            challenges supports challenges inconclusive

Click on any point to see details.
```

## Components

### EvidenceTimeline
Main timeline view showing:
- Horizontal or vertical timeline
- Confidence at each point
- Direction of change (up/down)
- Test power rating
- Click for details

### ConfidenceChart
Line chart showing confidence over time:
- X-axis: time/events
- Y-axis: confidence (0-100)
- Annotations at key events

### EvidenceDetailPopover
On click, show:
- Full test description
- Predictions vs observation
- Interpretation
- Source/citation

## Data for Visualization
```typescript
interface TimelinePoint {
  entryId: string;
  timestamp: Date;
  confidenceBefore: number;
  confidenceAfter: number;
  result: 'supports' | 'challenges' | 'inconclusive';
  testPower: 1 | 2 | 3 | 4 | 5;
  testSummary: string;
}
```

## File Location
`apps/web/src/components/brenner-loop/evidence/EvidenceTimeline.tsx`
`apps/web/src/components/brenner-loop/evidence/ConfidenceChart.tsx`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Timeline shows all evidence events
- [ ] Confidence changes visualized clearly
- [ ] Detail view on click
- [ ] Works with many entries
